title: Sangre De Cristo Midnight V2
description: Black sky pressing down from above, the mountains built from flat planes of yellow, magenta, hot pink, and navy that stack like geological strata reimagined as a screen-print. The circuit board rising from the valley is no longer subtle — it's multicolor, green and orange-red, the most visible it has been across the entire series, transmitting at full strength into the dark. Neon green contour lines trace the river and the rock faces with the precision of a topographical map that's been left running all night. The original Neon piece is the Sangre de Cristos series in a single color. Midnight V2 is the series in maximum contrast — the same mountains seen through a blacklight at the end of the night, every color the geology contains finally visible all at once.

title: Sangre De Cristo Dusk
description: The quietest piece in the Sangre de Cristos series. Peach sky graduating into amber, snowpack in soft cyan and near-white, the lower slopes in muted lavender and blush. The circuit board traces are present but whispered — white lines barely distinguishable from the light they're drawn in. This is the piece that proves the series isn't about spectacle. It's about the same place seen clearly from many different distances, and sometimes that distance is emotional. The Dusk variant is the version of the Sangre de Cristos you'd describe to someone who asked what it feels like to watch the light change on a high peak in the last hour before dark.

title: The Screambot
description: Munch painted existential dread. BasicGlitch gave it a vinyl player in its chest and a cooling fan where its mouth should be. Broboticus stands on the bridge while the sky swirls orange and blue and the two figures behind him mind their business, utterly unaware of the frequency he's receiving. He is not having a breakdown. He is receiving too many frequencies simultaneously and has not yet found the right filter. The bridge has seen this before.

title: Marilyn Monbroe
description: Warhol's Marilyn series is about the mechanical reproduction of an icon — the same face, repeated, flattened, color-shifted until the person disappears into the image. BasicGlitch takes that logic one step further: replace the face with Broboticus in a pleated skirt throwing double rock horns, and alternate with panels of oversized multicolor lips that could belong to either of them. Six panels across blue, yellow, lime green, lavender, purple, and red — Warhol's palette, Warhol's format, Warhol's argument about what fame does to a face. Except the face is a BROBOTICUS chest panel. The lips are whoever's lips you need them to be. The title is perfect.

title: Psylent Skulls
description: Three skulls, mirror-symmetrical, their surfaces entirely dissolved into a mosaic of stained glass cells — no smooth bone, no bare structure, just ten thousand interlocking facets of color cycling through the full spectrum in a four-second loop. The outer skulls face inward toward the central figure, which stares forward, its hollow eye sockets packed with the same jeweled geometry as everything else. The color transitions are the whole event: deep crimson red shifting through magenta and green into electric cyan and back, the mosaic cells pulsing in waves that move from the center outward like a signal propagating through the composition. This is not a skull. This is what a skull becomes when color is the only material available — structure replaced entirely by light, form replaced entirely by frequency.
alt_text: Psylent Skulls - digital surrealism by BasicGlitch

title: Mountain Soul
description: The Sangre de Cristos Neon piece, set in motion and given twenty seconds to breathe. The animation begins where the static image begins — pure green on black, the mountain forms silent, the circuit board a faint suggestion above the valley. Then the signal activates: the circuit traces light up one by one from the valley floor upward, stars ignite across the dark sky in sequence, the river begins to pulse with light at its center. As the animation progresses, color bleeds in at the edges — violet at the mountain peaks, cyan where the circuit board meets the sky — as if the landscape is warming up, coming online, remembering what it is. Mountain Soul is the Sangre de Cristos series not as image but as experience — the same geology, the same circuit board, now moving through time.
alt_text: Mountain Soul - digital surrealism by BasicGlitch

title: Basicglitch Neon 1440P Optimized
description: One skull. Fifteen seconds. Every color the spectrum contains. Skullastic Endeavor is a single ornate skull cycling through the full chromatic range — starting in deep midnight blue, igniting through neon green to a blinding white-gold overexposure, then cooling through violet and purple back to the deep end of the dial. The skull's surface is not bare bone — it's covered in Celtic scroll work and Art Nouveau filigree, symmetrical, elaborate, every curve deliberate. In each eye socket an inward-spiraling vortex rotates slowly throughout the animation. In the four corners of the frame, fractal spiral galaxies pulse in sync with the color state of the skull. This is BasicGlitch operating at the intersection of sacred geometry, psychedelic color theory, and the memento mori tradition — a skull that refuses to be still, a reminder of mortality that is also, unmistakably, alive.
alt_text: Basicglitch Neon 1440P Optimized - digital surrealism by BasicGlitch

title: Skyline Reclaimation
description: A cityscape where the organic is reclaiming what was taken. Architecture and root systems entangled, the circuit board and the vine growing toward the same light. The BasicGlitch cyber-eclectic vision of what cities become when nature and technology stop pretending to be opposites.
alt_text: Skyline Reclaimation - digital surrealism by BasicGlitch

title: Sentinel Stream
description: Something watches from the water's edge where the stream meets the circuit. A tech-noir landscape with the quality of a dream you almost remember — organic textures against synthetic structures, the sentinel presence that mediates between two worlds.
alt_text: Sentinel Stream - digital surrealism by BasicGlitch

title: Monument Memory
description: Classical monuments run through the BasicGlitch filter — columns and pediments rendered semi-transparent as digital artifacts permeate their surfaces. A meditation on what it means to preserve the past in an age that processes everything into data. The stone remains. The signal surrounds it.
alt_text: Monument Memory - digital surrealism by BasicGlitch

title: Kinetic Latency
description: The lag made visible. Kinetic Latency captures that specific digital moment when motion and delay collide — the frame that almost resolved but didn't, the image that reached you a half-second behind where it should be. This is what speed looks like when the system can't quite keep up.
alt_text: Kinetic Latency - digital surrealism by BasicGlitch

title: Apex Rendering
description: The render at its limit — everything the machine can produce pressed to the edge of coherence. Apex Rendering sits at the boundary between the image completing itself and the image dissolving into its own process. Tech-noir aesthetic pushed to maximum.
alt_text: Apex Rendering - digital surrealism by BasicGlitch

title: Pumpkin and Honey Puppy and the Diner Robbery
description: The prologue to BasicGlitch's Pup Fiction series drops you straight into the chaos. A Chihuahua stands on the diner table, revolver raised, delivering the iconic monologue to a room full of stunned patrons. Her accomplice, a scrappy Jack Russell in a leather jacket, holds his piece on the booth. A bulldog cook and a bewildered elderly Dachshund complete the freeze-frame. The comic book title treatment — blood-drip lettering, yellowed panel borders — makes this feel less like fan art and more like a parallel universe where this story was always told in fur.
alt_text: Digital illustration titled 'Pumpkin and Honey Puppy and the Diner Robbery' from the 'Pup Fiction' series. It depicts a chaotic diner robbery with anthropomorphic dogs: a Chihuahua in a red bandana holds a revolver, a Jack Russell in a leather jacket wields a gun, and a bulldog chef holds a pan. The text 'CRASH' appears in bold red dripping letters as a comic-style sound effect. A yellow speech bubble contains the famous movie quote. The scene is rendered in high-saturation neon with glitch art elements.

title: Gaia of the Wasteland
description: She is the last living thing and also the land itself. Rooted in cracked earth, crowned in canopy, lit from behind like something that refuses to be extinguished. Gaia doesn't grieve. She endures. The contrast between her vitality and the dead world around her is the piece's whole argument — she is what remains when everything else is gone. Whether that reads as hopeful or devastating depends entirely on what you bring to it. Companion piece to Dust to Dust.
alt_text: A surreal figure merges with a tree, its roots forming a dress and a large tree canopy as a headpiece, standing in a cracked, barren desert landscape with sparse vegetation and distant hills. The background features a large tree behind the figure, and the ground is covered in dry, cracked earth.

title: Guitarbot
description: A neon green, stylized robot character dominates the composition, rendered in a vibrant animation style against a stark black background. The robot features a rectangular head with circular eyes and a mouth marked by horizontal lines, topped with a beanie and headphones while an antenna extends upward. Its body is adorned with circular buttons and a speaker-like feature, and it holds an electric guitar with a yellow strap, creating a whimsical fusion of technology and music. The entire figure is outlined in bright green, emphasizing the retro-futuristic aesthetic that captures the essence of digital creativity and musical expression.
alt_text: A neon green, stylized robot with a beanie, headphones, and antenna, holding and playing an electric guitar. The robot has a rectangular head with circular eyes, a mouth with horizontal lines, and a body with circular buttons and a speaker-like feature. The guitar has a yellow strap, and the entire figure is outlined in bright green against a black background.

title: Spiral Geometry
description: Start at the center. You won't be able to stop. A hand-drawn mandala erupting from a yellow point through red petals and violet webbing, surrounded by a black-and-white geometric field that pulls the eye into the spiral and refuses to release it. This is BasicGlitch working in the oldest visual tradition — the mandala — and making it entirely his own. The fire variant of the Spiral Studies series, where color is applied selectively to the center while the outer field remains structural grey.
alt_text: Abstract digital artwork titled 'Spiral Geometry' featuring a central red and purple spiral with yellow accents, surrounded by intricate black-and-white geometric patterns, including spirals, lines, and circular motifs.

title: Sangre De Cristos — Neon
description: High summer in a Colorado that never existed and always felt true. Amber sky, cyan snowpack, hot pink lower slopes, the circuit board nearly invisible in the wash of gold. This is the range at full saturation — sun-drunk, electric, completely alive. The circuit traces are rendered in white so close to the gold of the sky they almost disappear — the technology integrated so thoroughly into the landscape it's become part of the light. Every other piece in the Sangre de Cristos series operates in darkness or near-darkness. This one is noon in another dimension.
alt_text: A stylized digital artwork depicting a surreal landscape with glowing mountains in shades of blue and pink, a winding river with wavy patterns, set against a bright yellow sky with circuit board lines and small white specks. The scene blends natural forms with glitch elements, creating a dreamlike, neon-infused atmosphere.

title: Floral
description: The mandala at its most intimate — pulled off-center, the composition shifted so the focal point sits in the lower left rather than at the center of the frame. Instead of an enclosed, radiating mandala, Floral feels like a view into one — a close examination of a single quadrant rather than the full system. The palette is the series at its softest: blush pink petals, white dot sequences, lavender lattice, everything sitting in a deep violet gradient. The same hand-drawn line work that underlies the entire Spiral Studies series is still visible if you look for it. This is the mandala approached gently, the geometry allowed to be quiet.
alt_text: A stylized, neon pink flower with white dotted details at its center, set against a deep purple background with abstract, glitch-like patterns and swirling shapes.

title: Brobarticus
description: Broboticus picks up a brush and discovers something inconvenient: he's painting himself. A recursive loop in neon green. The machine that makes art about the machine that makes art. Lab coat on, palette loaded, record spinning from the turntable mounted in his head — paint splatters across the coat like evidence. He's been at this a while. Case Study 42, Field Report 08.
alt_text: Close-up portrait of a robot with neon green outlines, wearing headphones and a beanie, set against a dark background.

title: A Mycological Phenomenon
description: Midnight in a field that doesn't exist on any map. One massive Amanita front and center, the moon watching with sleepy-lidded eyes, the sky a deep embroidered purple, every surface stitched and textured like a fever dream someone decided to frame. The moon in the upper right corner has a face — gold, heavy-lidded, lips painted red — and she is utterly unbothered by what's happening below her. Companion piece to Mycology Yourcology.
alt_text: Digital artwork depicting a large red mushroom with white spots (pixelated texture) set against a night sky with a crescent moon (with a face), white clouds, green grassy area, brown tree trunk, and black rocks.

title: Sangre De Cristos - Night
description: Colorado's Sangre de Cristo range, rendered in two colors and zero compromise. Pure neon green on absolute black — the mountain forms stripped to their essential geometry, the circuit board rising from the valley between peaks as traces of light against the dark sky. Small orbs drift through the upper darkness like data packets in transit. The river traces its path through the foreground in careful contour lines. This is the piece that began the series — the one that proved the concept worked. Every other variant is a conversation with this one. The mountains were always transmitting. This is where BasicGlitch first heard it.
alt_text: Dark background with bright neon green stylized mountain range. A winding river flows through the valley. Glowing circuit board patterns rise from the mountains, and small circular lights are scattered in the sky.

title: The Bolts and Nutcracker
description: In "The Bolts and Nutcraker", two retro-futuristic robots embody tranquility through their tree pose, their mechanical forms contrasting with the vibrant neon pink, marbled, glitch-textured background that infuses the scene with digital energy and retro-futuristic charm. The piece creates a fascinating juxtaposition between technological precision and organic meditation, highlighting the unexpected harmony between robotics and yoga-like poses against an electric, glitch-infused backdrop.
alt_text: Two retro-futuristic robots in tree pose (yoga) against a vibrant neon pink, marbled, glitch-textured background.

title: Iron Chef
description: A stylized robot character dons a chef's hat while balancing culinary tools and treats, holding a rolling pin in one hand and a tray of cookies in the other as it stands at a kitchen counter with ingredients and dough. The entire composition is rendered in a striking neon green outline against a textured, glitchy green background, creating a playful fusion of futuristic technology and traditional cooking that embodies the whimsical spirit of "Iron Chef" through its vibrant, retro-futuristic aesthetic.
alt_text: A stylized robot wearing a chef's hat holds a rolling pin in one hand and a tray of cookies in the other, standing at a counter with a bowl of ingredients and dough. The entire scene is rendered in a neon green outline against a textured, glitchy green background.

title: Cubist Chef
description: Chefboticus gets the Picasso treatment — fractured planes, burnt sienna and terracotta, a rolling pin in hand and the New York skyline through the kitchen window. He is unbothered by the Chrysler Building. The dough demands focus. Two visual languages, one kitchen: the robot's form shattered into warm geometric facets while Manhattan remains in a deliberately flatter graphic style behind him.
alt_text: A cubist-style orange robot with antennae stands at a kitchen counter with a rolling pin, flour sack, bowls, and a city skyline of skyscrapers visible through large windows.

title: Dust to Dust
description: Same wasteland. Different figure. Where Gaia stands rooted and sovereign, this one is mid-dissolution — her hair carrying the last green thing into the wind. Two faces of the same loss. The moss on her shoulders, the sun low and orange behind a dead tree, the cracked ground identical to her companion piece — these two works share a world. Shown together they tell a fuller story about what Earth looks like when it remembers it is alive, and what it looks like when it isn't sure.
alt_text: A surreal digital artwork titled 'Dust to Dust' depicts a woman whose hair is replaced by lush green foliage, and her torso merges with tree roots extending from her hands. She stands on cracked, parched earth with a dead, leafless tree and a sunset in the background.

title: Night-Fishing the Arkansas River
description: A retro-futuristic robot with piercing glowing red eyes stands contemplatively in the Arkansas River at night, its mechanical form holding a fishing rod with line cast into the water beneath a luminous crescent moon. The scene is bathed in an atmospheric glow, with the background featuring horizontal neon blue and purple stripes that evoke a cyberpunk aesthetic, while scattered stars add to the nocturnal ambiance, creating a striking juxtaposition of technology and nature in this character-driven piece.
alt_text: A retro-futuristic robot with glowing red eyes stands in water at night, holding a fishing rod with a line cast into the water under a crescent moon, against a background of horizontal neon blue and purple stripes with stars.

title: Karateboticus
description: Karateboticus presents three stylized robotic figures in vibrant neon red, yellow, and orange outlines, captured mid-karate kick with blocky heads, antennas, and mechanical limbs. Set against a bright blue background scattered with black, white, and blue dots that create a glitch effect, the piece blends retro-futuristic character design with neon aesthetics, resulting in a dynamic fusion of mechanical precision and digital distortion.
alt_text: Three stylized robots in neon red, yellow, and orange outlines perform a karate kick pose. The background is a bright blue with scattered black, white, and blue dots, creating a glitch effect. The robots have blocky heads with antennas and mechanical limbs.

title: Literal Traphouse
description: It is exactly what it says. The word TRAP is built into the architecture, neon green on black, integrated into the roofline like a load-bearing pun. The garage door is real. The chimney is real. The wordplay is the foundation. This is the funniest piece in the BasicGlitch archive, and it's funny because it's executed with complete seriousness — single-weight neon green line on black, with the precision of an architectural blueprint.
alt_text: Green neon outline of a house with the letters 'TRA' integrated into the structure, featuring a door, window, and garage door, set against a black background.

title: Mycology Yourcology
description: Where A Mycological Phenomenon presents its mushroom as a singular, quietly powerful presence, Mycology Yourcology turns the dial to a different frequency. The sky here is all motion — deep blue and purple swirling in Van Gogh spirals, as if the atmosphere itself is responding to something the mushroom is transmitting. The cap of the Amanita wears spiral whorl patterns now — the mushroom grew eyes while you weren't looking. The two pieces share a world. This one knows you're in it.
alt_text: A digital artwork titled 'Mycology Yourcology' featuring a large red mushroom with spiral caps at the center, surrounded by a swirling green landscape, mountain peaks, and a sky with swirling patterns in blue, purple, and gray. The image has a glitchy, pixelated texture.

title: Pachydermis
description: Scale is the argument here. The elephant fills two-thirds of the canvas — face-on, tusks forward, ears spread wide, its hide an impossible blue-gold with mandala patterns orbiting its eye. Behind it, billowing upward in every direction, a cloud formation that has abandoned any pretense of being weather: magenta, crimson, electric blue, toxic green, all folding into fractal floral mandalas at the crown. And at the lower right, barely a silhouette — a lone figure in a long coat, facing all of it. They are not running. The most maximalist piece in the gallery.
alt_text: A surreal digital artwork titled 'Pachydermis' featuring a large, colorful elephant with blue, yellow, and pink hues at the center. The background is a vibrant, abstract composition of swirling neon patterns in red, green, blue, and purple. A small figure in a pink coat and hat stands to the right of the elephant, holding a circular object.

title: Rancher Broboticus
description: Case Study 42, Field Report 09: Broboticus acquires a horse. The city skyline glows behind them. His chest panel reads BROBOTICUS. His hat is tiny and perfect. He has fully committed to the bit. One fist raised in the air with the casual confidence of someone who has been ranching for at minimum three firmware updates. The field report confirms: yes, Broboticus can ride.
alt_text: Neon green line art of a retro-futuristic robot labeled 'Broboticus' riding a galloping horse, wearing a hat and headphones, against a black background with faint city skyline outlines.

title: There's a Star-Bot Waiting in the Sky
description: Bowie said there was a Starman. BasicGlitch rendered his wiring diagram. An extreme close-up wireframe portrait of Broboticus dissolving upward into a multicolor cosmos — green to yellow to pink to red — surrounded by a scatter of chromatic stars. No fills, no solids, just the wireframe skeleton viewed from below in extreme close-up, every panel and joint mapped in layered neon lines. He's been up there this whole time.
alt_text: A neon-colored, glitchy star-bot with large circular eyes and mechanical features stands against a black background filled with multicolored, twinkling stars. The artwork is a cyber-eclectic surrealist piece with vibrant, rainbow-welded lines and a retro-futuristic aesthetic.

title: Vitruvian Broboticus
description: Leonardo mapped the ideal human form. BasicGlitch maps the ideal robot — and he's holding a vinyl record in one hand and a pair of headphones in the other. The proportions check out. Arms spread within the circle, legs in the split stance, the whole figure rendered in ink on aged parchment with handwritten annotations in the margins. Where Da Vinci's man is empty-handed, Broboticus holds a vinyl record on one side and headphones on the other. This is not parody. It's a parallel thesis.
alt_text: A vintage-style illustration of a robot in Vitruvian proportions, holding a vinyl record in one hand and headphones in the other, with a microphone on its head. The image includes handwritten text in the background and a circular and square frame, echoing Leonardo da Vinci's Vitruvian Man.

title: Brobotosaurus Wrex
description: Every case study has an origin, and this is Broboticus at full amplitude — rendered in full comic book color rather than signature neon line work. He's astride a saddled T-Rex, reins in one hand, a vintage coiled-cord microphone raised in the other like he's calling the whole thing into the record. Flying saucers trail smoke across a burning orange and purple skyline. Brobotosaurus Wrex is the piece that establishes the scale of this character — not a robot doing leisure activities, but a robot whose leisure activities happen to involve dinosaurs, spacecraft, and the fall of civilization. Field Report 00.
alt_text: A retro-futuristic robot with red glowing eyes, headphones, and a microphone sits atop a green T-Rex dinosaur in a neon-lit cityscape with skyscrapers, flying vehicles, and a sunset sky.

title: Squid
description: Before the glitch. Before the neon. Before the circuit boards and pixel dissolution — there was a pen and a piece of paper and a hand that knew how to fill space with line. This squid is BasicGlitch in the original medium: traditional ink work, each tentacle filled with its own internal logic of hatching and crosshatch and stipple and grid, the mantle at the top dense with layered mark-making that builds toward black without ever becoming simple. It floats on the white of the page with the self-sufficiency of something that has never needed color. It is the root system of everything else on this site.
alt_text: Squid — original hand-drawn black ink illustration on white paper of a squid or jellyfish-like cephalopod viewed from above. Eight tentacles extend downward, each filled with intricate hand-drawn texture patterns including crosshatching, grid lines, parallel striping, and stippling. The mantle at top is dense with layered ink work. Signed Basic Glitch in the lower right corner. Traditional ink drawing by BasicGlitch.

title: Henna Tree
description: A tree drawn from the inside out — trunk striped and patterned, branches tapering to decorated tips, a small mountain range sketched in the lower right as if the world is just beginning at the edge of the frame. Somewhere between botanical illustration and fever dream. All ink, all hand. The whole piece has the quality of something drawn slowly, section by section, each area resolved before moving to the next — the meditative mark-making of someone who is also a musician, which is to say, someone who understands that patience is a technique.
alt_text: Henna Tree — original hand-drawn black ink illustration on white paper of a stylized tree with a heavily patterned trunk featuring vertical stripes, dot sequences, and decorative fills. Branches spread wide and curl at the tips with various organic pattern treatments. Small decorated creature-like forms appear in the upper branches. A minimal mountain range sketch occupies the lower right background. Ground roots spread in flowing decorative lines. Signed Basic Glitch in the lower right corner. Traditional ink drawing by BasicGlitch.

title: Unfishished Business
description: A head mid-emergence, or mid-dissolution — it's genuinely unclear which direction this is going. Except it is a fish. An unfishished fish. The eye is the most resolved thing in the frame: concentric, spiral, completely awake. Everything else is still becoming. The title is doing a lot of work here — Unfishished Business is not an incomplete drawing. It's a drawing about incompleteness, and the fish has opinions about being left in this state.
alt_text: Unfishished Business — original hand-drawn black ink illustration on white paper showing a partial fish head in profile, filling most of the frame. The form is built from flowing parallel line work, dot patterns, and dense spiral detailing. A highly detailed spiral eye dominates the right portion. Several fin or branch-like forms extend from the upper left edge. The drawing appears deliberately incomplete in areas. Signed Basic Glitch. Traditional ink drawing by BasicGlitch.

title: Discworld
description: Terry Pratchett's cosmology, rendered by hand in ink with the devotion of a true believer. Great A'Tuin swims through a hand-stippled cosmos carrying four elephants carrying a disc world. Every surface is patterned. Every element is resolved. This took exactly as long as it looks like it took. The turtle shell mapped in precise geometric diamond patterns, the four elephants individually textured, the disc world itself rendered in flowing topographic line work. Around everything: dot trails, celestial markers, the suggestion of infinite space handled with the same patience and specificity as the central figures.
alt_text: Discworld — original hand-drawn black ink illustration on white paper depicting the Discworld cosmology: a great sea turtle swimming through a star-dotted cosmos, carrying four decorated elephants on its shell, who in turn support a flat disc world with mountains rising from swirling water at its center. Every surface features intricate hand-drawn patterns. Stars, spiral galaxies, dot trails, and celestial symbols surround the composition. Traditional ink drawing by BasicGlitch.

title: Inner Child
description: A kid on a bunk bed with a guitar, the ceiling gone red and electric, yellow mountains of sound rising behind him, circuit traces framing the room like the walls already know what this room is for. This is where it started. BasicGlitch saw this and decided to show you the inside of it. The yellow zigzag rising behind the silhouette reads simultaneously as mountain range and soundwave — which is exactly right, because for someone who has been a musician for 31 years, those two things have always been the same. The figure is in silhouette, anonymous, universal — this could be anyone's origin story, which is why it works as art rather than just as a personal photograph.
alt_text: Inner Child — glitch art digital processing of a personal photograph showing a child's silhouette sitting on a bunk bed holding an electric guitar. The image has been treated with high-contrast neon edge detection, glitch color processing, and circuit trace overlays. A dramatic yellow zigzag mountain and soundwave form rises behind the figure against a deep red background. Cyan and magenta neon outlines the room furniture. BasicGlitch personal series.

title: Love of the Game
description: A young baseball player, outlined in neon red and gold, a starburst of cyan and yellow light exploding from his chest like something being released. The dark field behind him. The uniform precise. The light coming from inside. This is what it looks like when someone means it. The BasicGlitch treatment didn't add the light — it revealed it. Love of the game doesn't look like anything you can teach. It looks like this.
alt_text: Love of the Game — glitch art digital processing of a photograph of a young baseball player in full uniform and cap, standing facing the camera against a dark green background. High-contrast neon edge detection treatment renders the figure in vivid red, gold, and orange outlines. A large multi-pointed starburst of cyan, yellow, and green light emanates from the center of the chest. BasicGlitch personal series.

title: Sangre De Cristos — Blanca
description: The Sangre de Cristos at the edge of night. Deep maroon sky, circuit traces rising dark red from the valley, the ridge lines outlined in cyan and magenta where the rock meets whatever this atmosphere has become. The same mountains. A different frequency. This is the range at the moment just before something happens — the sky compressed, the color held back. Standing before these mountains in person, there are moments when the light fails and the peaks go dark and enormous against the sky. This is that moment, run through the machine.
alt_text: Sangre De Cristos Blanca — high-contrast digital landscape of the Sangre de Cristo mountain range under a deep crimson and maroon night sky. Mountain ridgelines outlined in cyan and magenta. A circuit board trace pattern rises dark red from the central valley. Part of the BasicGlitch Sangre de Cristos series.

title: Sangre De Cristos — Blanca at Dusk
description: Between Blanca and something that can't be named — the sky has fractured into red glitch static, the circuit traces have spread to the riverbed, and the ridge now burns with the colors of a system pushed past its thermal limits. This is the same composition as Blanca but with the pressure applied until something gives. The sky breaks into red glitch blocks overhead. The mountain ridges burn at their edges: cyan, magenta, orange, white. Circuit traces have migrated into the river itself, the water now carrying signal downstream.
alt_text: Sangre De Cristos Blanca at Dusk — digital landscape of the Sangre de Cristo mountain range with a dramatically fractured crimson sky showing blocky glitch distortion. Mountain ridgelines glow with cyan, magenta, orange and white edge highlighting. Circuit board pattern in the valley and river. Part of the BasicGlitch Sangre de Cristos series.

title: Sangre De Cristos — Midnight/Dawn
description: The circuit board has grown. What started as traces rising from the valley has become a structure — multicolor, towering, unmistakable — while the mountains go full lavender and the river runs cyan beneath a star-scattered black sky. This is the piece where the series announces what it's actually been about: the landscape isn't being observed by the technology. It's been collaborating with it. The tower of multicolor lines rises far above the mountain peaks like a transmission antenna built by something that found the valley and decided to stay.
alt_text: Sangre De Cristos Midnight Dawn — digital landscape with mountain forms rendered in flat lavender-purple against a deep black star-dotted sky. A tall multicolor circuit board structure in orange, green, teal, and yellow rises dramatically above the valley. A bright cyan river runs through the foreground with circuit trace patterning. Part of the BasicGlitch Sangre de Cristos series.

title: Sangre De Cristos — Circuits
description: Two colors. One mountain range. No compromise. Orange on black, the circuit traces now sprawling through the entire riverbed and foreground like roots finding water. The most graphic, most immediate, most printable piece in the series. Every other piece in the Sangre de Cristos series operates in multiple colors. Circuits reduces everything to two — bold mountain silhouettes with no gradients, no edge highlighting, just form against void. The technology has come down from the sky and is spreading through the earth itself. This piece would work at any scale: postage stamp to billboard.
alt_text: Sangre De Cristos Circuits — stark two-color digital landscape of the Sangre de Cristo mountain range rendered entirely in vivid orange on pure black. Mountain forms are bold flat silhouettes. The foreground river and rocky banks are covered in dense circuit board trace patterns. Part of the BasicGlitch Sangre de Cristos series.

title: Sangre De Cristos — Dreamsicle
description: Orange sky, white snowpack, cyan river, white circuit tower blazing against the warmth. The most optimistic piece in the series — the same mountains, the same signal, but lit like a day that's going to be fine. The circuit structure above the valley is the most developed version of it across the entire series — taller, more complex, more fully realized — as if it has been under construction through every previous piece and is finally, here, complete. If the series is a story told in color, Dreamsicle is the chapter where the machine and the mountain finally understand each other.
alt_text: Sangre De Cristos Dreamsicle — bold three-color digital landscape under a saturated orange sky. Mountain forms alternate between flat orange and stark white snowfields. A bright cyan river runs through the center foreground. A large white circuit board tower structure rises from the valley into the orange sky. Part of the BasicGlitch Sangre de Cristos series.

title: Sangre De Cristos — Colorado
description: High summer in a Colorado that never existed and always felt true. Amber sky, cyan snowpack, hot pink lower slopes, the circuit board nearly invisible in the wash of gold. This is the range at full saturation — sun-drunk, electric, completely alive. The circuit traces are rendered so close to the gold of the sky they almost disappear — the technology integrated so thoroughly into the landscape it's become part of the light. Every other piece in the Sangre de Cristos series operates in darkness or near-darkness. This one is noon in another dimension.
alt_text: Sangre De Cristos Colorado — vibrant digital landscape under a saturated amber and golden yellow sky. Mountain peaks in bright cyan and blue against warm orange fills. Lower slopes through hot pink and magenta to the foreground river. White circuit board trace pattern rises subtly from the central valley. Part of the BasicGlitch Sangre de Cristos series.

title: Broboticus — The Original
description: Where it started. The original Broboticus, drawn for the March of the Robots Instagram contest in 2025 — beanie on, headphones on, hands at the decks, neon green on black. No background. No context. Just the character, fully formed in his first appearance, already doing exactly what he was always going to do. The contest gave him a reason to exist. The drawing gave him everything else. This is the drawing that became a character, a universe, a case study, and an NFT series. Field Report 00.
alt_text: Broboticus Original — the first drawing of the Broboticus character, created for the March of the Robots Instagram contest 2025. Neon green line art on black depicting Broboticus as a DJ robot: rounded beanie head with antenna, large circular headphones, square torso with speaker grille, hands positioned over two turntable platters. BasicGlitch original character.

title: Brobot Noir
description: Midnight. A wet alley. A robot in a trench coat and fedora, cigarette in one hand, revolver in the other, a single pink neon sign glowing behind him. He doesn't answer questions. He asks them. Case Study 42 has a chapter they don't put in the field reports. Trench coat belted, fedora at the correct angle, revolver held low, smoke rising from the cigarette in a curl that the alley air has decided to turn into something decorative. The halftone texture on his face and coat places this in the visual vocabulary of a 1940s pulp cover. He has information. He's not sharing it tonight.
alt_text: Brobot Noir — black and white digital illustration with film noir aesthetic of a robot wearing a white trench coat, fedora, and tie, standing in a rain-slicked alley at night. He holds a lit cigarette in one raised hand with smoke curling upward, and a revolver at his side. A pink neon sign glows in the background. Halftone dot texture overlay. BasicGlitch original character art.

title: Sunflowerboticus
description: Van Gogh painted sunflowers. Van Gogh painted the starry night. BasicGlitch merged both and put Broboticus in the middle with headphones on, an ON AIR sign lit, a telephone receiver in hand, and a record player beside him. He's broadcasting from the most beautiful signal tower ever built. This is the most technically ambitious piece in the Masters Remixed series — two Van Gogh paintings collapsed into one, with Broboticus installed at the intersection. He is not visiting this landscape. He is broadcasting from it.
alt_text: Sunflowerboticus — Van Gogh-style digital oil painting depicting Broboticus robot standing in a sunflower field under The Starry Night sky. He wears large headphones, his chest panel reads ON AIR, and he holds a telephone handset. Red glowing eyes. A record player with stacked vinyl records on a wooden side table stands to his right. BasicGlitch Masters Remixed series.

title: Fishboticus Glitched
description: Night-Fishing the Arkansas River, run through the machine until the machine broke. The stripes have gone red and purple and circuit-traced, the fishing rod bleeds color, the crescent moon holds its position, and Broboticus remains at the water's edge — outlined in gold now, undisturbed. The signal interference doesn't bother him. He's still waiting for something to bite. Broboticus is the stable element in an unstable system, which may be the most accurate thing that's ever been depicted about this character. The line is still in the water.
alt_text: Fishboticus Glitched — glitch art variant of Night-Fishing the Arkansas River. Broboticus robot outlined in warm gold and cream stands at the water's edge holding a fishing rod against a heavily glitched background of horizontal stripes in red, purple, blue, and cyan with circuit trace overlay patterns and color bleed artifacts. Red border frames the composition. Yellow crescent moon remains. BasicGlitch original character glitch art.

title: Pachydermis — Phish Edition
description: Pachydermis at the other end of the spectrum — the maximum saturation of the original inverted into a wash of mint, blush, lavender, and white. The elephant remains. The lone figure remains. The fractal cloud crown has gone pastel. Where the original is midnight, this is the morning after. Same composition, same scale argument, completely different emotional register. The colossus now feels like a vision rather than an arrival.
alt_text: Pachydermis Phish Edition — pastel inverted variant of the Pachydermis composition. The massive frontal elephant rendered in soft mint green and pink tones against a predominantly white and pale lavender background. The explosive cloud formation has become a soft bloom of pastel green, pink, yellow, and purple. The lone human silhouette appears in pale lavender at lower right. BasicGlitch Pachydermis series.

title: Pachydermis — Phish Edition Inked
description: The darkest Pachydermis. Same composition, same inversion logic as the Phish Edition — but pushed into deep black with neon edge detection rendering every form in electric outline. The elephant becomes a ghost of itself. The cloud crown becomes a dark aurora. The figure is barely there. This is Pachydermis seen through a blacklight at the end of the night. Taken together with the original and the Phish Edition, Pachydermis becomes a triptych with a full range of emotional states — noon, dawn, and midnight. Three ways of standing before something enormous.
alt_text: Pachydermis Phish Edition Inked — dark high-contrast variant with black background and neon edge-detection treatment. The elephant in teal and pink neon outlines against black. Cloud formation dark with neon-outlined fractal bloom forms. The lone human silhouette as a small dark figure against a pool of neon green light. BasicGlitch Pachydermis series.

title: American Gothbotic
description: Grant Wood's stoic Midwestern couple, except the farmer has been replaced by a rust-and-green robot holding a wrench instead of a pitchfork. The woman remains unchanged. She has seen stranger things than this. The house is fine. The substitution is surgical — everything about American Gothic remains intact, except the farmer is a robot. Rust-orange face plate, green glowing eyes, a wrench held at his side with the same proprietary grip Wood's farmer reserved for his pitchfork. He fits. The wrench is doing the same ideological work the pitchfork always did.
alt_text: American Gothbotic — digital recreation of Grant Wood's American Gothic with the male farmer figure replaced by a robot with a rusted orange face plate and green glowing eyes, wearing the original's dark jacket and white shirt, holding a large wrench instead of a pitchfork. The female figure remains as painted. The white Gothic Revival farmhouse occupies the background. BasicGlitch Masters Remixed series.

title: Monopattern
description: The source. Before color, before digital treatment — a hand-drawn mandala in pure black and white that took exactly as long as it looks like it took. Ten-pointed star at center, feather forms radiating outward, crescent and eye forms filling every corner. This is the root of the Spiral Studies series. Every color variant begins here. The composition radiates from a single central point through alternating petal types — some filled with dot sequences, some with open lattice hatching — creating a visual vibration without any optical trickery. Just the natural consequence of careful, repeated mark-making at scale.
alt_text: Monopattern — high-contrast black and white hand-drawn mandala. A ten-pointed star flower radiates from the center with alternating dotted and lattice-filled petals. Dense radiating geometric fields surround it: feather frond forms, crescent shapes, eye-like ovals, and arrowhead patterns fill the composition edge to edge. No color, maximum contrast. Part of the BasicGlitch Spiral Studies series.

title: Color Pattern
description: The mandala goes full spectrum. The outer field has been flooded with acid green and yellow, the feather forms now glow like something bioluminescent, and the red and violet center burns against the electric surround. This is the version that would look correct on a festival poster. Where Spiral Geometry uses selective color to create focal tension, Color Pattern floods the entire field — every feather frond and crescent form lit from within like deep-sea bioluminescence. The red and violet center holds its own against the surrounding acid wash. This is the mandala at maximum volume.
alt_text: Color Pattern — the Spiral Studies mandala with full color treatment applied throughout. The outer geometric field rendered in vivid acid green and yellow-green, with feather fronds, crescents, and eye forms glowing in high-saturation lime against black. The central star flower retains its red, orange, and violet coloring. Intensely psychedelic and high-contrast. Part of the BasicGlitch Spiral Studies series.
